diagrams.title: Three Levels of Emotional Design (Don Norman)
diagrams.description: Visual representation of the three levels of emotional design showing progression from immediate reaction to long-term reflection
elements: Behavioral Level (During Use) - Functionality, usability, experience
Reflective Level (After Use) - Memories, long-term feelings, meaning
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how emotions evolve during user-product interaction

diagrams.title: SAM Model Dimensions
diagrams.description: 3D representation of Self-Assessment Manikin for measuring emotional responses
elements: Dominance Axis: Controlled to In Control
diagrams.purpose: Measuring and visualizing user emotional responses to interfaces

diagrams.title: Emotional Design Impact Pyramid
diagrams.description: Hierarchy showing how emotional design impacts different aspects from individual satisfaction to business success
elements: Base: Visual Aesthetics → Middle: User Satisfaction → Top: Brand Loyalty → Apex: Business Success
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the cascading benefits of emotional design investments

diagrams.title: User Emotional Journey Map
diagrams.description: Line graph showing emotional state fluctuation throughout user journey with a product
elements: X-axis: Journey Stages (Discovery, Onboarding, Core Use, Retention)
diagrams.purpose: Identifying opportunities for emotional enhancement throughout user experience
